blocks.text: +----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+| FINDINGS - "JUMP BALLS" IN THE AWARD DATA                                              For discussion  -  2026-06-15 || Which multi-source supplier lanes are genuinely in play (contestable), vs many-vendor-but-locked                     |+----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+| CORPUS  Full-history FSRS/SAM.gov first-tier subawards (supplier-role)   |   VA $4,343.6M . Columbia $3,342.2M       ||         . DDG-51 $3,095.4M   |   132 (PIID x work-type) lanes . 2,897 vendor-lane rows   |   FSRS floors             |+--------------------------------------+---------------------------------------+---------------------------------------+| JUMP BALL 1                          | JUMP BALL 2                           | TIMING & WHAT DRIVES IT               || Multi-source lanes up for re-buy     | Single -> multi (a grain finding)     | Prime calendar does NOT drive re-buy  || ------------------------------------ | ------------------------------------- | ------------------------------------- || SCREEN: >=2 active recent vendors    | Strict '1 vendor -> 2+' ~never fires  | Dist. from supplier award-burst to    ||   + others' share > a rounding tail  | at the work-type-lane grain: a lane   | the PIID prime base award is DIFFUSE: ||   (splits genuine vs locked lanes)   | rolls up dozens of components.        | VA   median  942d  ( 9% within 180d)  ||                                      | Absence != 'no second-sourcing' --    | COL  median  511d  (22% within 180d)  || HEADLINE LANES (recent supplier $)   | it is a GRAIN ARTIFACT.               | DDG  median 1162d  (11% within 180d)  || VA  structural 33v  t1=29% $296M     |                                       |                                       || VA  piping     56v  t1=24% $208M     | USABLE SIGNAL = falling Top-1 share   | => re-sourcing rides the shipbuilder  || VA  coatings   10v  t1=34% $159M     | + recent 2nd entry, incumbent ACTIVE  |    -> supplier clock, NOT the Navy    || COL piping     60v  t1=29% $498M     |                                       |    -> prime block clock.              || COL structural 40v  t1=30% $420M     | SPLITS (incumbent active = real):     | Re-buy = last award + median gap      || DDG piping     32v  t1=12% $139M     | COL castings   Scot Forge  96->69%    | between subaward bursts (cadence).    || DDG structural 31v  t1=26% $120M     | DDG machining  Rolls-Royce 90->42%    |                                       || DDG electrical  9v  t1=25% $159M     | DDG piping     CIRCOR     100->32%    | RECOMMENDATION                        ||                                      | DDG electrical Leonardo   100->36%    | 1) Use Source Concentration as the    || COUNTER-EXAMPLE (screen earns keep)  |                                       |    loosening screen.                  || VA electrical: 21v but t1=93%        | SWAPS / WIND-DOWN (incumbent gone =   | 2) Drill top candidates to the        || -> NOT flagged: many names, one      | not jump balls):                      |    capability (component) tier for    ||    locked incumbent (NG turbine      | COL coatings   Globe 64->25%,         |    named same-item 1->2 stories.      ||    generators). Raw count misleads.  |                silent since Nov 2023  |                                       |+--------------------------------------+---------------------------------------+---------------------------------------+| METHOD  Re-buy window = last award + median gap between subaward bursts; the Prime Awards tab is context only (2/39  ||         PIIDs absent from FPDS; many base dates sit at the 2017/2018 pull floor).                                    || OPEN    Thresholds (others'-share >=10%; Top-1 fall / entry) are explicit constants -- adjust and re-run. Recent     ||         window FY22->present incl. partial FY26 to catch entrants.                                                   || SOURCE  methodology_jump_balls_20260615.md; workbook tabs Re-buy Timing / Source Concentration / Lane Vendors x FY / ||         Prime Awards; research/extracted/jumpball_prime_clustering.csv.                                              |+----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+

runs.text: | FINDINGS - "JUMP BALLS" IN THE AWARD DATA                                              For discussion  -  2026-06-15 |
| Which multi-source supplier lanes are genuinely in play (contestable), vs many-vendor-but-locked                     |
| CORPUS  Full-history FSRS/SAM.gov first-tier subawards (supplier-role)   |   VA $4,343.6M . Columbia $3,342.2M       |
|         . DDG-51 $3,095.4M   |   132 (PIID x work-type) lanes . 2,897 vendor-lane rows   |   FSRS floors             |
| JUMP BALL 1                          | JUMP BALL 2                           | TIMING & WHAT DRIVES IT               |
| Multi-source lanes up for re-buy     | Single -> multi (a grain finding)     | Prime calendar does NOT drive re-buy  |
| SCREEN: >=2 active recent vendors    | Strict '1 vendor -> 2+' ~never fires  | Dist. from supplier award-burst to    |
|   + others' share > a rounding tail  | at the work-type-lane grain: a lane   | the PIID prime base award is DIFFUSE: |
|   (splits genuine vs locked lanes)   | rolls up dozens of components.        | VA   median  942d  ( 9% within 180d)  |
|                                      | Absence != 'no second-sourcing' --    | COL  median  511d  (22% within 180d)  |
| HEADLINE LANES (recent supplier $)   | it is a GRAIN ARTIFACT.               | DDG  median 1162d  (11% within 180d)  |
| VA  structural 33v  t1=29% $296M     |                                       |                                       |
| VA  piping     56v  t1=24% $208M     | USABLE SIGNAL = falling Top-1 share   | => re-sourcing rides the shipbuilder  |
| VA  coatings   10v  t1=34% $159M     | + recent 2nd entry, incumbent ACTIVE  |    -> supplier clock, NOT the Navy    |
| COL piping     60v  t1=29% $498M     |                                       |    -> prime block clock.              |
| COL structural 40v  t1=30% $420M     | SPLITS (incumbent active = real):     | Re-buy = last award + median gap      |
| DDG piping     32v  t1=12% $139M     | COL castings   Scot Forge  96->69%    | between subaward bursts (cadence).    |
| DDG structural 31v  t1=26% $120M     | DDG machining  Rolls-Royce 90->42%    |                                       |
| DDG electrical  9v  t1=25% $159M     | DDG piping     CIRCOR     100->32%    | RECOMMENDATION                        |
|                                      | DDG electrical Leonardo   100->36%    | 1) Use Source Concentration as the    |
| COUNTER-EXAMPLE (screen earns keep)  |                                       |    loosening screen.                  |
| VA electrical: 21v but t1=93%        | SWAPS / WIND-DOWN (incumbent gone =   | 2) Drill top candidates to the        |
| -> NOT flagged: many names, one      | not jump balls):                      |    capability (component) tier for    |
|    locked incumbent (NG turbine      | COL coatings   Globe 64->25%,         |    named same-item 1->2 stories.      |
|    generators). Raw count misleads.  |                silent since Nov 2023  |                                       |
| METHOD  Re-buy window = last award + median gap between subaward bursts; the Prime Awards tab is context only (2/39  |
|         PIIDs absent from FPDS; many base dates sit at the 2017/2018 pull floor).                                    |
| OPEN    Thresholds (others'-share >=10%; Top-1 fall / entry) are explicit constants -- adjust and re-run. Recent     |
|         window FY22->present incl. partial FY26 to catch entrants.                                                   |
| SOURCE  methodology_jump_balls_20260615.md; workbook tabs Re-buy Timing / Source Concentration / Lane Vendors x FY / |
|         Prime Awards; research/extracted/jumpball_prime_clustering.csv.                                              |

blocks.text: Worktype mix | Outsourced scope concentrates in piping, structural, and coatings.[ stacked column: outsourced $ by worktype, Virginia / Columbia / DDG-51 ]Three worktypes hold ~70% of outsourced scope.- piping and structural lead every yard- coatings rises post-FY26 on Columbia- electrical stays concentrated in one incumbentSource: FSRS/SAM.gov first-tier subawards; workbook tab Worktype Shares.
